import heapq

def merge_k_sorted_arrays(arrays):
    """
    Merge k sorted arrays into a single sorted array.

    Args:
        arrays: A list of k sorted arrays.

    Returns:
        A single sorted list containing all elements from the input arrays.
    """
    merged_list = []
    min_heap = [] # Stores tuples of (value, array_index, element_index)

    # Initialize the min-heap with the first element from each non-empty array
    for i, arr in enumerate(arrays):
        if arr:  # Only add if the array is not empty
            heapq.heappush(min_heap, (arr[0], i, 0))

    while min_heap:
        value, array_idx, element_idx = heapq.heappop(min_heap)
        merged_list.append(value)

        # If there are more elements in the same array, add the next one to the heap
        if element_idx + 1 < len(arrays[array_idx]):
            next_element = arrays[array_idx][element_idx + 1]
            heapq.heappush(min_heap, (next_element, array_idx, element_idx + 1))

    return merged_list

if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Test cases
    arrays1 = [
        [1, 5, 9],
        [2, 6, 10],
        [3, 7, 11]
    ]
    print(f"Merging {arrays1}: {merge_k_sorted_arrays(arrays1)}") # Expected: [1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 7, 9, 10, 11]

    arrays2 = [
        [10, 20, 30],
        [15, 25],
        [5, 12, 18, 35]
    ]
    print(f"Merging {arrays2}: {merge_k_sorted_arrays(arrays2)}") # Expected: [5, 10, 12, 15, 18, 20, 25, 30, 35]

    arrays3 = [
        [],
        [1],
        [2, 4],
        [3]
    ]
    print(f"Merging {arrays3}: {merge_k_sorted_arrays(arrays3)}") # Expected: [1, 2, 3, 4]

    arrays4 = [
        [1, 2, 3]
    ]
    print(f"Merging {arrays4}: {merge_k_sorted_arrays(arrays4)}") # Expected: [1, 2, 3]

    arrays5 = [
        [],
        [],
        []
    ]
    print(f"Merging {arrays5}: {merge_k_sorted_arrays(arrays5)}") # Expected: []
